name: Portugal
description: Portugal is a western European country on the Atlantic, long favoured by remote workers and retirees for its mild coast, English-friendly cities, and residence routes. The figures below are screened, sourced, and dated. They are not advice.

priceLevelIndexEU27.sourceName: Eurostat, price level index for actual individual consumption (tec00120, EU27=100)
priceLevelIndexEU27.excerpt: Portugal price level index for actual individual consumption (EU27 = 100): 86.6 in 2025 (100 = EU average).

averageJanuaryHighC.excerpt: Lisbon, the capital, shown as representative for Portugal: mean daily maximum 14.8 C in January (1981 to 2010 normals).

averageJulyHighC.excerpt: Lisbon: mean daily maximum 27.9 C in July (1981 to 2010 normals).

averageAnnualSunHours.excerpt: Lisbon: about 2,874.9 mean annual sunshine hours (1981 to 2010 normals).

koppenClass.sourceName: GloH2O Koppen-Geiger climate classification (Beck et al. 2023)
koppenClass.excerpt: Koppen-Geiger class Csa, hot-summer Mediterranean, across much of central and southern Portugal.

digitalNomadVisa.sourceName: AIMA, Portugal D8 remote-work residence (Nomadas Digitais)
digitalNomadVisa.excerpt: Portugal D8 digital nomad visa: minimum income of 4 times the national minimum wage (RMMG 920 euros, Decreto-Lei 139/2025), about 3,680 euros per month in 2026, assessed by AIMA at the appointment date.

goldenVisa.value: Real estate and capital-transfer routes removed in October 2023 (Mais Habitacao, Law 56/2023). Remaining routes include 500,000 euros into qualifying Portuguese investment funds, 250,000 euros cultural support (200,000 in low-density areas), 500,000 euros scientific research, and job creation.
goldenVisa.sourceName: Portugal golden visa (ARI) after the 2023 Mais Habitacao reform
goldenVisa.excerpt: Real estate purchases and capital transfers no longer qualify; investment funds (minimum 500,000 euros) and cultural support (from 250,000 euros) remain.

headlinePersonalIncomeTaxRate.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Portugal, taxes on personal income
headlinePersonalIncomeTaxRate.excerpt: Top personal income tax rate 48 percent on taxable income above about 86,634 euros, plus a solidarity rate of 2.5 to 5 percent on higher incomes.

specialRegime.value: IFICI (the NHR successor, from 2024): 20 percent flat on eligible Portuguese employment and professional income, plus exemptions on certain foreign income. The old NHR regime closed to new entrants at the end of 2023.
specialRegime.sourceName: PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries, Portugal, significant developments
specialRegime.excerpt: IFICI provides a special 20 percent rate on category A and B income from eligible activities; applicants must not have benefited from the NHR regime.

physiciansPer1000.sourceName: WHO Global Health Observatory, medical doctors per 10,000, via Wikipedia
physiciansPer1000.excerpt: Portugal: 57.7 medical doctors per 10,000 (about 5.8 per 1,000), WHO 2021. Counts doctors licensed to practise, which overstates practising doctors.

peaceIndexScore.sourceName: Institute for Economics and Peace, Global Peace Index 2024
peaceIndexScore.excerpt: Portugal 2024 Global Peace Index score 1.372 (rank 7 of 163), one of the most peaceful countries. Lower is more peaceful.
